category|Category:Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja}} [[JA:姉倉比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11447234}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=December 26, 2022 (Mon) 03:14 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Anekurahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Anekura-hime-jinja Toyama City Kureha Town 2014.8.25 - panoramio (1).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shinjō-chō 2-chōme 13-47 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Ōniikawa-hime-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}
Sugawara no Michizane |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Listed Shrine in the National Histories|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} / Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = April 19 / October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Niikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Anekurahime Shrine''' (あねくらひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Anekura-hime Shrine'''. The shrine is built atop an ancient burial mound (kofun). There are two shrines of the same name within Toyama City ({{ill|lt=Kureha Town (Toyama City)|Q86731852|WD=Q86731852|lt=Kureha-hime origin shrine|1=Kureha-hime origin shrine}} and Funakura). Both are considered candidate shrines (ronsha) of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} “Anekurahime Shrine of {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}}, {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}}.” Both formerly held the rank of {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. According to a synthesis of the shrine traditions of both shrines, Anekurahime-no-Kami subdued bandits in the region, established her residence on Mount Funakura, ruled the area, and spread agriculture, sericulture, and weaving among the local people. In the “Sentatsuroku,” Anekurahime-no-Kami was married to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Noto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Isurugi-hiko Shrine|Q11381009|WD=Q11381009|1=Isurugi Hikojinja}}). However, Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami entered into a relationship with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Mount Senbokuyama (the enshrined deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). Enraged, Anekurahime-no-Kami hurled stones from Mount Funakura to attack Noto-hime-no-Kami. Anekurahime-no-Kami’s younger sister, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, also joined the attack, plunging the land of Koshi into great turmoil. {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} of Izumo was dispatched to Koshi by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami-musubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and together with five assembled deities, he suppressed the rebellion. As punishment for causing the disturbance, Anekurahime-no-Kami was stripped of her territory and exiled to Kureha Kotake, where she was ordered to teach weaving to the local women. Fukurahime-no-Kami received the same punishment. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and the others then attacked the remaining two deities and ultimately executed Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami and Noto-hime-no-Kami on the seashore. The place name “Nei District” (婦負郡) is derived from Anekurahime-no-Kami, and the place name “Kureha” is also associated with weaving. At the Kureha Anekurahime Shrine, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} are additionally enshrined as principal deities. === Overview === ;Kureha-hime origin shrine *Location – 1813 Kureha Town, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 111.. *Shrine grounds – 3,950 tsubo (formerly located on a keyhole-shaped tumulus) :It is said to be the oldest shrine in Etchū Province. Nearby, it served as a base for {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}’s march to the capital ({{ill|lt=jōraku|Q10867576|WD=Q10867576|1=Joraku}}). In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 7), 200 koku of shrine lands were donated, and on {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}}, {{ill|lt=1546|Q6533|WD=Q6533|1=1546}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), a written prayer was dedicated to the shrine. On {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|WD=Q2729|1=July 17}}, {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5), the Tanaka Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), the main hall was rebuilt, and on {{ill|lt=May 11|Q2558|WD=Q2558|1=May 11}}, {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), the Shinmei Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), the shrine buildings were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 30), a new protective roof over the sanctuary was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=1978|Q2483|WD=Q2483|1=1978}} (Shōwa 53), the worship hall underwent major repairs. (“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,” p. 112.) ;Funakura *Location – 2360 Funakura, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 157.. *Shrine grounds – 13,157 tsubo :Founded during the {{ill|lt=Taihō era|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|1=Taihō (era)}} (when the monk Kifuku established the Shingon Buddhist temple Jōryū-ji). After Jōryū-ji burned down during the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era, the shrine is said to have been established on its former site. On {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}}, {{ill|lt=1906|Q2049|WD=Q2049|1=1906}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 39), it was listed as a formerly designated shrine. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja|Anekurahime Shrine (Kureha Town)}} * [http://anekurahime.jp/ Anekurahime Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Default sort: Anekurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama City]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Uesugi Kenshin]] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * あねくらひめじんじゃ * アネクラ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Commons category (P373) == * Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anekura- Shrine|lt=Anekura- Shrine|WD=Q135040380|1=Anekura- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県富山市呉羽町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja}} [[JA:姉倉比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11447234}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=December 26, 2022 (Mon) 03:14 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Anekurahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Anekura-hime-jinja Toyama City Kureha Town 2014.8.25 - panoramio (1).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shinjō-chō 2-chōme 13-47 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Ōniikawa-hime-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}
Sugawara no Michizane |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Listed Shrine in the National Histories|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} / Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = April 19 / October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Niikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Anekurahime Shrine''' (あねくらひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Anekura-hime Shrine'''. The shrine is built atop an ancient burial mound (kofun). There are two shrines of the same name within Toyama City ({{ill|lt=Kureha Town (Toyama City)|Q86731852|WD=Q86731852|lt=Kureha-hime origin shrine|1=Kureha-hime origin shrine}} and Funakura). Both are considered candidate shrines (ronsha) of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} “Anekurahime Shrine of {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}}, {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}}.” Both formerly held the rank of {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. According to a synthesis of the shrine traditions of both shrines, Anekurahime-no-Kami subdued bandits in the region, established her residence on Mount Funakura, ruled the area, and spread agriculture, sericulture, and weaving among the local people. In the “Sentatsuroku,” Anekurahime-no-Kami was married to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Noto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Isurugi-hiko Shrine|Q11381009|WD=Q11381009|1=Isurugi Hikojinja}}). However, Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami entered into a relationship with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Mount Senbokuyama (the enshrined deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). Enraged, Anekurahime-no-Kami hurled stones from Mount Funakura to attack Noto-hime-no-Kami. Anekurahime-no-Kami’s younger sister, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, also joined the attack, plunging the land of Koshi into great turmoil. {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} of Izumo was dispatched to Koshi by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami-musubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and together with five assembled deities, he suppressed the rebellion. As punishment for causing the disturbance, Anekurahime-no-Kami was stripped of her territory and exiled to Kureha Kotake, where she was ordered to teach weaving to the local women. Fukurahime-no-Kami received the same punishment. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and the others then attacked the remaining two deities and ultimately executed Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami and Noto-hime-no-Kami on the seashore. The place name “Nei District” (婦負郡) is derived from Anekurahime-no-Kami, and the place name “Kureha” is also associated with weaving. At the Kureha Anekurahime Shrine, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} are additionally enshrined as principal deities. === Overview === ;Kureha-hime origin shrine *Location – 1813 Kureha Town, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 111.. *Shrine grounds – 3,950 tsubo (formerly located on a keyhole-shaped tumulus) :It is said to be the oldest shrine in Etchū Province. Nearby, it served as a base for {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}’s march to the capital ({{ill|lt=jōraku|Q10867576|WD=Q10867576|1=Joraku}}). In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 7), 200 koku of shrine lands were donated, and on {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}}, {{ill|lt=1546|Q6533|WD=Q6533|1=1546}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), a written prayer was dedicated to the shrine. On {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|WD=Q2729|1=July 17}}, {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5), the Tanaka Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), the main hall was rebuilt, and on {{ill|lt=May 11|Q2558|WD=Q2558|1=May 11}}, {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), the Shinmei Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), the shrine buildings were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 30), a new protective roof over the sanctuary was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=1978|Q2483|WD=Q2483|1=1978}} (Shōwa 53), the worship hall underwent major repairs. (“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,” p. 112.) ;Funakura *Location – 2360 Funakura, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 157.. *Shrine grounds – 13,157 tsubo :Founded during the {{ill|lt=Taihō era|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|1=Taihō (era)}} (when the monk Kifuku established the Shingon Buddhist temple Jōryū-ji). After Jōryū-ji burned down during the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era, the shrine is said to have been established on its former site. On {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}}, {{ill|lt=1906|Q2049|WD=Q2049|1=1906}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 39), it was listed as a formerly designated shrine. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja|Anekurahime Shrine (Kureha Town)}} * [http://anekurahime.jp/ Anekurahime Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Default sort: Anekurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama City]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Uesugi Kenshin]] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]]

Shrine|WD=Q135040380|1=Anekura- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県富山市呉羽町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja}} [[JA:姉倉比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11447234}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=December 26, 2022 (Mon) 03:14 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Anekurahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Anekura-hime-jinja Toyama City Kureha Town 2014.8.25 - panoramio (1).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shinjō-chō 2-chōme 13-47 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Ōniikawa-hime-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}
Sugawara no Michizane |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Listed Shrine in the National Histories|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} / Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = April 19 / October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Niikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Anekurahime Shrine''' (あねくらひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Anekura-hime Shrine'''. The shrine is built atop an ancient burial mound (kofun). There are two shrines of the same name within Toyama City ({{ill|lt=Kureha Town (Toyama City)|Q86731852|WD=Q86731852|lt=Kureha-hime origin shrine|1=Kureha-hime origin shrine}} and Funakura). Both are considered candidate shrines (ronsha) of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} “Anekurahime Shrine of {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}}, {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}}.” Both formerly held the rank of {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. According to a synthesis of the shrine traditions of both shrines, Anekurahime-no-Kami subdued bandits in the region, established her residence on Mount Funakura, ruled the area, and spread agriculture, sericulture, and weaving among the local people. In the “Sentatsuroku,” Anekurahime-no-Kami was married to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Noto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Isurugi-hiko Shrine|Q11381009|WD=Q11381009|1=Isurugi Hikojinja}}). However, Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami entered into a relationship with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Mount Senbokuyama (the enshrined deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). Enraged, Anekurahime-no-Kami hurled stones from Mount Funakura to attack Noto-hime-no-Kami. Anekurahime-no-Kami’s younger sister, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, also joined the attack, plunging the land of Koshi into great turmoil. {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} of Izumo was dispatched to Koshi by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami-musubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and together with five assembled deities, he suppressed the rebellion. As punishment for causing the disturbance, Anekurahime-no-Kami was stripped of her territory and exiled to Kureha Kotake, where she was ordered to teach weaving to the local women. Fukurahime-no-Kami received the same punishment. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and the others then attacked the remaining two deities and ultimately executed Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami and Noto-hime-no-Kami on the seashore. The place name “Nei District” (婦負郡) is derived from Anekurahime-no-Kami, and the place name “Kureha” is also associated with weaving. At the Kureha Anekurahime Shrine, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} are additionally enshrined as principal deities. === Overview === ;Kureha-hime origin shrine *Location – 1813 Kureha Town, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 111.. *Shrine grounds – 3,950 tsubo (formerly located on a keyhole-shaped tumulus) :It is said to be the oldest shrine in Etchū Province. Nearby, it served as a base for {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}’s march to the capital ({{ill|lt=jōraku|Q10867576|WD=Q10867576|1=Joraku}}). In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|WD=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 7), 200 koku of shrine lands were donated, and on {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}}, {{ill|lt=1546|Q6533|WD=Q6533|1=1546}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), a written prayer was dedicated to the shrine. On {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|WD=Q2729|1=July 17}}, {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5), the Tanaka Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), the main hall was rebuilt, and on {{ill|lt=May 11|Q2558|WD=Q2558|1=May 11}}, {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), the Shinmei Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), the shrine buildings were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 30), a new protective roof over the sanctuary was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=1978|Q2483|WD=Q2483|1=1978}} (Shōwa 53), the worship hall underwent major repairs. (“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,” p. 112.) ;Funakura *Location – 2360 Funakura, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 157.. *Shrine grounds – 13,157 tsubo :Founded during the {{ill|lt=Taihō era|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|1=Taihō (era)}} (when the monk Kifuku established the Shingon Buddhist temple Jōryū-ji). After Jōryū-ji burned down during the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era, the shrine is said to have been established on its former site. On {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}}, {{ill|lt=1906|Q2049|WD=Q2049|1=1906}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 39), it was listed as a formerly designated shrine. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja|Anekurahime Shrine (Kureha Town)}} * [http://anekurahime.jp/ Anekurahime Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Default sort: Anekurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama City]] [[Category:Uesugi Kenshin]] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]]

Shrine|qid=Q135040380|1=Anekura- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県富山市呉羽町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja}} [[JA:姉倉比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11447234}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=December 26, 2022 (Mon) 03:14 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Anekurahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Anekura-hime-jinja Toyama City Kureha Town 2014.8.25 - panoramio (1).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|qid=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|qid=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shinjō-chō 2-chōme 13-47 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Ōniikawa-hime-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}
Sugawara no Michizane |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Listed Shrine in the National Histories|Q118304363|qid=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} / Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = April 19 / October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Niikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Anekurahime Shrine''' (あねくらひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|qid=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|qid=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Anekura-hime Shrine'''. The shrine is built atop an ancient burial mound (kofun). There are two shrines of the same name within Toyama City ({{ill|lt=Kureha Town (Toyama City)|Q86731852|qid=Q86731852|lt=Kureha-hime origin shrine|1=Kureha-hime origin shrine}} and Funakura). Both are considered candidate shrines (ronsha) of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} “Anekurahime Shrine of {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|qid=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}}, {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|qid=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}}.” Both formerly held the rank of {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. According to a synthesis of the shrine traditions of both shrines, Anekurahime-no-Kami subdued bandits in the region, established her residence on Mount Funakura, ruled the area, and spread agriculture, sericulture, and weaving among the local people. In the “Sentatsuroku,” Anekurahime-no-Kami was married to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Noto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Isurugi-hiko Shrine|Q11381009|qid=Q11381009|1=Isurugi Hikojinja}}). However, Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami entered into a relationship with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} of Mount Senbokuyama (the enshrined deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noto-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). Enraged, Anekurahime-no-Kami hurled stones from Mount Funakura to attack Noto-hime-no-Kami. Anekurahime-no-Kami’s younger sister, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukurahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, also joined the attack, plunging the land of Koshi into great turmoil. {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} of Izumo was dispatched to Koshi by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami-musubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and together with five assembled deities, he suppressed the rebellion. As punishment for causing the disturbance, Anekurahime-no-Kami was stripped of her territory and exiled to Kureha Kotake, where she was ordered to teach weaving to the local women. Fukurahime-no-Kami received the same punishment. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and the others then attacked the remaining two deities and ultimately executed Isurugi-hiko-no-Kami and Noto-hime-no-Kami on the seashore. The place name “Nei District” (婦負郡) is derived from Anekurahime-no-Kami, and the place name “Kureha” is also associated with weaving. At the Kureha Anekurahime Shrine, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} are additionally enshrined as principal deities. === Overview === ;Kureha-hime origin shrine *Location – 1813 Kureha Town, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 111.. *Shrine grounds – 3,950 tsubo (formerly located on a keyhole-shaped tumulus) :It is said to be the oldest shrine in Etchū Province. Nearby, it served as a base for {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|qid=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}’s march to the capital ({{ill|lt=jōraku|Q10867576|qid=Q10867576|1=Joraku}}). In {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|qid=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 7), 200 koku of shrine lands were donated, and on {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|qid=Q2359|1=February 26}}, {{ill|lt=1546|Q6533|qid=Q6533|1=1546}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), a written prayer was dedicated to the shrine. On {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|qid=Q2729|1=July 17}}, {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|qid=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5), the Tanaka Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), the main hall was rebuilt, and on {{ill|lt=May 11|Q2558|qid=Q2558|1=May 11}}, {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), the Shinmei Shrine was transferred and jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|qid=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), the shrine buildings were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|qid=Q18577|1=1955}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 30), a new protective roof over the sanctuary was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=1978|Q2483|qid=Q2483|1=1978}} (Shōwa 53), the worship hall underwent major repairs. (“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,” p. 112.) ;Funakura *Location – 2360 Funakura, Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture“Toyama Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” (November 10, 1983), edited and published by the Toyama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, p. 157.. *Shrine grounds – 13,157 tsubo :Founded during the {{ill|lt=Taihō era|Q1346269|qid=Q1346269|1=Taihō (era)}} (when the monk Kifuku established the Shingon Buddhist temple Jōryū-ji). After Jōryū-ji burned down during the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era, the shrine is said to have been established on its former site. On {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|qid=Q2524|1=April 20}}, {{ill|lt=1906|Q2049|qid=Q2049|1=1906}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 39), it was listed as a formerly designated shrine. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kureha-Anekurahime-jinja|Anekurahime Shrine (Kureha Town)}} * [http://anekurahime.jp/ Anekurahime Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Default sort: Anekurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama City]] [[Category:Uesugi Kenshin]] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kureha-Anekurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]]
